concepts.label: Ecosystem
concepts.definition: A biological community of interacting organisms and their physical environment

concepts.label: Biome
concepts.definition: A large naturally occurring community of flora and fauna occupying a major habitat

concepts.label: Tropical Rainforest
concepts.definition: Dense forest biome near the equator with high rainfall (>2000mm/year) and biodiversity

concepts.label: Temperate Deciduous Forest
concepts.definition: Forest biome with moderate climate where trees shed leaves seasonally

concepts.label: Temperate Rainforest
concepts.definition: Cool, wet forest biome found in coastal regions with heavy rainfall

concepts.label: Boreal Forest
concepts.definition: Taiga biome dominated by coniferous trees in subarctic regions

concepts.label: Tropical Dry Forest
concepts.definition: Forest biome with distinct wet and dry seasons in tropical regions

concepts.label: Mangrove Forest
concepts.definition: Coastal forest ecosystem of salt-tolerant trees in tropical and subtropical tidal areas

concepts.label: Cloud Forest
concepts.definition: Tropical or subtropical montane forest frequently immersed in low-level cloud cover

concepts.label: Mediterranean Forest
concepts.definition: Biome with hot dry summers and cool wet winters, sclerophyll vegetation

concepts.label: Tropical Savanna
concepts.definition: Grassland biome with scattered trees in tropical regions with seasonal rainfall

concepts.label: Temperate Grassland
concepts.definition: Biome of grasses and herbs with hot summers, cold winters, moderate rainfall

concepts.label: Alpine Grassland
concepts.definition: High-altitude grassland above treeline with low-growing vegetation

concepts.label: Steppe
concepts.definition: Semi-arid grassland biome with short grasses and extreme temperature variation

concepts.label: Prairie
concepts.definition: North American temperate grassland with deep fertile soils

concepts.label: Pampas
concepts.definition: South American fertile lowland grassland in Argentina, Uruguay, Brazil

concepts.label: Veld
concepts.definition: Southern African grassland biome at various elevations

concepts.label: Hot Desert
concepts.definition: Arid biome with extreme heat, <250mm annual rainfall, sparse vegetation

concepts.label: Cold Desert
concepts.definition: Arid biome with cold winters and low precipitation, found at high latitudes/altitudes

concepts.label: Coastal Desert
concepts.definition: Desert biome along coastlines influenced by cold ocean currents

concepts.label: Semi-Arid Desert
concepts.definition: Transitional biome between desert and grassland with 250-500mm rainfall

concepts.label: Sahara Desert
concepts.definition: World's largest hot desert covering 9.2 million km² in North Africa

concepts.label: Gobi Desert
concepts.definition: Large cold desert in northern China and southern Mongolia

concepts.label: Atacama Desert
concepts.definition: Hyper-arid coastal desert in Chile, driest non-polar place on Earth

concepts.label: Kalahari Desert
concepts.definition: Semi-arid sandy savanna in southern Africa covering 900,000 km²

concepts.label: Tundra
concepts.definition: Treeless biome with permafrost, found in Arctic and high mountain regions

concepts.label: Alpine Tundra
concepts.definition: Tundra found above treeline on mountains at any latitude

concepts.label: Antarctic Tundra
concepts.definition: Tundra ecosystem on Antarctic Peninsula and sub-Antarctic islands

concepts.label: Taiga
concepts.definition: Subarctic biome of coniferous forests, largest terrestrial biome by area

concepts.label: Chaparral
concepts.definition: Shrubland biome with hot dry summers and mild wet winters

concepts.label: Maquis
concepts.definition: Mediterranean shrubland of dense evergreen shrubs, equivalent to chaparral

concepts.label: Fynbos
concepts.definition: Unique shrubland biome in South Africa's Cape region, extremely species-rich

concepts.label: Heathland
concepts.definition: Open habitat dominated by low-growing ericaceous shrubs

concepts.label: Moorland
concepts.definition: Open upland habitat with acidic soil, heather, and sphagnum moss

concepts.label: Wetland
concepts.definition: Ecosystem where water covers soil or is near the surface, at least seasonally

concepts.label: Freshwater Marsh
concepts.definition: Wetland with non-tidal standing water and emergent vegetation

concepts.label: Salt Marsh
concepts.definition: Coastal wetland flooded by tidal saltwater with halophytic vegetation

concepts.label: Swamp
concepts.definition: Forested wetland with standing or slow-moving water

concepts.label: Bog
concepts.definition: Acidic peat-accumulating wetland fed by precipitation, nutrient-poor

concepts.label: Fen
concepts.definition: Peat-forming wetland fed by groundwater, less acidic than bogs

concepts.label: Peatland
concepts.definition: Wetland with thick layer of organic soil (peat), major carbon store

concepts.label: Floodplain
concepts.definition: Flat area alongside a river that is subject to periodic flooding

concepts.label: Riparian Zone
concepts.definition: Interface between land and a river or stream

concepts.label: Estuary
concepts.definition: Semi-enclosed coastal water body where freshwater meets saltwater

concepts.label: Lagoon
concepts.definition: Shallow body of water separated from the sea by a barrier

concepts.label: Coral Reef
concepts.definition: Underwater ecosystem built by calcium carbonate-secreting corals

concepts.label: Kelp Forest
concepts.definition: Underwater ecosystem dominated by large brown algae (kelp) in cool waters

concepts.label: Seagrass Meadow
concepts.definition: Underwater ecosystem of flowering plants in shallow coastal waters

concepts.label: Deep Sea Hydrothermal Vent
concepts.definition: Ecosystem around volcanic ocean floor vents supporting chemosynthetic life

concepts.label: Cold Seep
concepts.definition: Deep-sea ecosystem where hydrogen sulfide and methane seep from the seafloor

concepts.label: Pelagic Zone
concepts.definition: Open water column of the ocean, away from shore and bottom

concepts.label: Epipelagic Zone
concepts.definition: Sunlit ocean zone 0-200m depth, photic zone supporting photosynthesis

concepts.label: Mesopelagic Zone
concepts.definition: Twilight ocean zone 200-1000m depth with dim light

concepts.label: Bathypelagic Zone
concepts.definition: Dark ocean zone 1000-4000m depth, no sunlight penetrates

concepts.label: Abyssopelagic Zone
concepts.definition: Deep ocean zone 4000-6000m depth, near-freezing temperatures

concepts.label: Hadopelagic Zone
concepts.definition: Deepest ocean zone >6000m in ocean trenches

concepts.label: Benthic Zone
concepts.definition: Ecological region at the lowest level of a body of water

concepts.label: Neritic Zone
concepts.definition: Shallow ocean zone over the continental shelf, 0-200m depth

concepts.label: Oceanic Zone
concepts.definition: Deep open ocean beyond the continental shelf

concepts.label: Intertidal Zone
concepts.definition: Coastal area between high and low tide marks

concepts.label: Subtidal Zone
concepts.definition: Coastal zone always submerged below the low tide mark

concepts.label: Aphotic Zone
concepts.definition: Deep ocean region where no sunlight penetrates, below ~1000m

concepts.label: Photic Zone
concepts.definition: Upper ocean layer where sunlight is sufficient for photosynthesis

concepts.label: Freshwater Lake
concepts.definition: Standing body of fresh water in a depression, important habitat

concepts.label: Oligotrophic Lake
concepts.definition: Deep, clear lake with low nutrient levels and high oxygen

concepts.label: Eutrophic Lake
concepts.definition: Shallow, murky lake with high nutrients and algal productivity

concepts.label: River Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Flowing freshwater ecosystem from headwaters to mouth

concepts.label: Vernal Pool
concepts.definition: Temporary seasonal pool important for amphibian breeding

concepts.label: Cave Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Subterranean habitat with no light, supporting specialized fauna

concepts.label: Urban Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Human-dominated ecosystem in cities with modified habitats

concepts.label: Agroecosystem
concepts.definition: Ecosystem managed for agricultural production

concepts.label: Montane Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Mountain ecosystem with altitudinal zonation of habitats

concepts.label: Island Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Isolated ecosystem with high endemism and vulnerability to invasion

concepts.label: Polar Ice Cap
concepts.definition: High-latitude ecosystem of permanent ice supporting specialized life

concepts.label: Continental Shelf
concepts.definition: Shallow underwater landmass extending from a continent, rich ecosystem

concepts.label: Mudflat
concepts.definition: Coastal wetland formed by deposition of mud, important for wading birds

concepts.label: Sand Dune Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Coastal or inland habitat of wind-deposited sand with pioneer vegetation

concepts.label: Oasis
concepts.definition: Fertile area in a desert where water table reaches the surface

concepts.label: Volcanic Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Habitat on or near active volcanoes with pioneer succession

concepts.label: Bamboo Forest
concepts.definition: Dense forest ecosystem dominated by bamboo species, especially in Asia

concepts.label: Gallery Forest
concepts.definition: Forest along a river in otherwise open landscape

concepts.label: Elfin Forest
concepts.definition: Stunted cloud forest at highest elevations with gnarled trees

concepts.label: Páramo
concepts.definition: High-altitude tropical grassland in the Andes above treeline

concepts.label: Cerrado
concepts.definition: Tropical savanna biome of Brazil, extremely biodiverse

concepts.label: Caatinga
concepts.definition: Semi-arid thorn scrub biome of northeastern Brazil

concepts.label: Pantanal
concepts.definition: World's largest tropical wetland in South America, ~150,000 km²

concepts.label: Sundarbans
concepts.definition: Largest mangrove forest in the world, shared by Bangladesh and India

concepts.label: Great Barrier Reef
concepts.definition: World's largest coral reef system off Australia, 2,300 km long

concepts.label: Amazon Rainforest
concepts.definition: Largest tropical rainforest covering 5.5 million km² in South America

concepts.label: Congo Rainforest
concepts.definition: Second largest tropical rainforest in central Africa

concepts.label: Borneo Rainforest
concepts.definition: Ancient tropical rainforest on Borneo, one of the oldest on Earth (~140 million years)

concepts.label: Daintree Rainforest
concepts.definition: Oldest tropical rainforest on Earth in Queensland, Australia (~180 million years)

concepts.label: Tongass National Forest
concepts.definition: Largest US national forest, temperate rainforest in Alaska

concepts.label: Soil
concepts.definition: Natural body of minerals, organic matter, water, and air supporting plant life

concepts.label: Alfisol
concepts.definition: Moderately weathered fertile soil with clay-enriched subsoil, forests and agriculture

concepts.label: Andisol
concepts.definition: Volcanic soil rich in amorphous minerals, very fertile

concepts.label: Aridisol
concepts.definition: Dry soil of arid regions with limited organic matter

concepts.label: Entisol
concepts.definition: Young soil with minimal profile development

concepts.label: Gelisol
concepts.definition: Permafrost soil found in polar and high-altitude regions

concepts.label: Histosol
concepts.definition: Organic soil (peat) with >20% organic matter content

concepts.label: Inceptisol
concepts.definition: Slightly developed soil showing beginning of horizon formation

concepts.label: Mollisol
concepts.definition: Dark, fertile grassland soil with thick humus-rich topsoil

concepts.label: Oxisol
concepts.definition: Deeply weathered tropical soil, low fertility, rich in iron and aluminum oxides

concepts.label: Spodosol
concepts.definition: Acidic soil with subsurface accumulation of organic matter and aluminum, conifer forests

concepts.label: Ultisol
concepts.definition: Heavily weathered acidic soil of warm humid regions with clay accumulation

concepts.label: Vertisol
concepts.definition: Clay-rich soil that shrinks and swells with moisture changes, deep cracks

concepts.label: Laterite
concepts.definition: Iron-rich hardened soil layer in tropical regions formed by intense weathering

concepts.label: Loess
concepts.definition: Fine wind-deposited silt soil, very fertile for agriculture

concepts.label: Alluvial Soil
concepts.definition: Fertile soil deposited by river flooding, excellent for agriculture

concepts.label: Chernozem
concepts.definition: Black soil of steppe regions, among the most fertile soils on Earth

concepts.label: Podzol
concepts.definition: Acidic leached soil typical of boreal forests with bleached subsurface layer

concepts.label: Terra Rossa
concepts.definition: Red clay soil formed by weathering of limestone in Mediterranean regions

concepts.label: Regolith
concepts.definition: Loose unconsolidated material overlying bedrock

concepts.label: Permafrost
concepts.definition: Permanently frozen ground for at least two consecutive years

concepts.label: Climate Change
concepts.definition: Long-term shift in global temperatures and weather patterns, accelerated by human activity

concepts.label: Global Warming
concepts.definition: Increase in Earth's average surface temperature due to greenhouse gas emissions

concepts.label: Greenhouse Effect
concepts.definition: Natural process where atmospheric gases trap heat, enhanced by human emissions

concepts.label: Carbon Dioxide
concepts.definition: Primary anthropogenic greenhouse gas (CO₂) from fossil fuel combustion

concepts.label: Methane
concepts.definition: Potent greenhouse gas (CH₄) with 80x CO₂ warming potential over 20 years

concepts.label: Nitrous Oxide
concepts.definition: Greenhouse gas (N₂O) from agriculture and industry, 273x CO₂ warming potential

concepts.label: Hydrofluorocarbons
concepts.definition: Synthetic greenhouse gases used in refrigeration, extremely potent warmers

concepts.label: Ozone Depletion
concepts.definition: Thinning of stratospheric ozone layer by CFCs and halons

concepts.label: Ozone Hole
concepts.definition: Severe ozone depletion over Antarctica discovered in 1985

concepts.label: Ocean Acidification
concepts.definition: Decrease in ocean pH due to absorption of excess atmospheric CO₂

concepts.label: Sea Level Rise
concepts.definition: Increase in mean sea level from thermal expansion and ice melt

concepts.label: Thermal Expansion
concepts.definition: Expansion of ocean water volume as temperature increases, major sea level factor

concepts.label: Glacier Retreat
concepts.definition: Reduction in glacier mass and extent due to warming temperatures

concepts.label: Ice Sheet Collapse
concepts.definition: Rapid disintegration of continental ice sheets (Greenland, Antarctica)

concepts.label: Arctic Sea Ice Decline
concepts.definition: Progressive reduction of Arctic sea ice extent and thickness

concepts.label: Coral Bleaching
concepts.definition: Expulsion of symbiotic algae from corals due to thermal stress

concepts.label: Desertification
concepts.definition: Land degradation in arid regions from climate change and human activities

concepts.label: Deforestation
concepts.definition: Permanent removal of forest cover for agriculture, logging, or development

concepts.label: Soil Erosion
concepts.definition: Removal of topsoil by wind, water, or tillage faster than formation

concepts.label: Soil Degradation
concepts.definition: Decline in soil quality through erosion, compaction, salinization, or pollution

concepts.label: Soil Salinization
concepts.definition: Accumulation of salts in soil from irrigation, reducing fertility

concepts.label: Soil Compaction
concepts.definition: Compression of soil reducing pore space, caused by heavy machinery

concepts.label: Biodiversity Loss
concepts.definition: Decline in species diversity and abundance at genetic, species, and ecosystem levels

concepts.label: Mass Extinction
concepts.definition: Loss of 75%+ of species in a geologically short period; sixth underway

concepts.label: Habitat Destruction
concepts.definition: Elimination of natural habitat rendering it unable to support native species

concepts.label: Habitat Fragmentation
concepts.definition: Breaking of continuous habitat into smaller isolated patches

concepts.label: Invasive Species
concepts.definition: Non-native organism causing ecological or economic harm in new environment

concepts.label: Overexploitation
concepts.definition: Harvesting species faster than they can reproduce

concepts.label: Overfishing
concepts.definition: Catching fish faster than populations can replenish

concepts.label: Bycatch
concepts.definition: Unintended capture of non-target species in fishing operations

concepts.label: Illegal Wildlife Trade
concepts.definition: Black market trade in endangered species and products worth billions annually

concepts.label: Poaching
concepts.definition: Illegal hunting or capturing of wild animals

concepts.label: Bushmeat Trade
concepts.definition: Commercial hunting and sale of wild animal meat, threatening tropical species

concepts.label: Pollution
concepts.definition: Introduction of harmful substances into the environment

concepts.label: Air Pollution
concepts.definition: Contamination of atmosphere with harmful gases and particles

concepts.label: Particulate Matter
concepts.definition: Fine airborne particles (PM2.5, PM10) harmful to respiratory health

concepts.label: Smog
concepts.definition: Air pollution from vehicle emissions and industry, photochemical or sulfurous

concepts.label: Ground-Level Ozone
concepts.definition: Harmful air pollutant formed by reaction of NOx and VOCs in sunlight

concepts.label: Sulfur Dioxide Pollution
concepts.definition: SO₂ from fossil fuel combustion causing acid rain and respiratory issues

concepts.label: Nitrogen Oxide Pollution
concepts.definition: NOx from combustion contributing to smog, acid rain, and ozone depletion

concepts.label: Volatile Organic Compounds
concepts.definition: Carbon-containing chemicals that evaporate easily, contributing to smog

concepts.label: Carbon Monoxide Pollution
concepts.definition: Toxic gas from incomplete combustion, colorless and odorless

concepts.label: Lead Pollution
concepts.definition: Toxic heavy metal contamination from paint, fuel, and industry

concepts.label: Mercury Pollution
concepts.definition: Toxic heavy metal entering environment from coal, mining, and industry

concepts.label: Arsenic Contamination
concepts.definition: Toxic metalloid in groundwater and soil from natural and industrial sources

concepts.label: Cadmium Pollution
concepts.definition: Toxic heavy metal from mining and phosphate fertilizers accumulating in food chain

concepts.label: Water Pollution
concepts.definition: Contamination of water bodies with harmful substances

concepts.label: Eutrophication
concepts.definition: Excessive nutrient enrichment of water causing algal blooms and oxygen depletion

concepts.label: Harmful Algal Bloom
concepts.definition: Rapid proliferation of toxin-producing algae in nutrient-rich water

concepts.label: Dead Zone
concepts.definition: Hypoxic area in water body where dissolved oxygen is too low for marine life

concepts.label: Acid Rain
concepts.definition: Precipitation with pH <5.6 from SO₂ and NOx emissions

concepts.label: Acid Mine Drainage
concepts.definition: Acidic water flowing from mining operations contaminating waterways

concepts.label: Oil Spill
concepts.definition: Release of petroleum into the environment, devastating to marine ecosystems

concepts.label: Plastic Pollution
concepts.definition: Accumulation of plastic waste in environment, especially oceans

concepts.label: Microplastic
concepts.definition: Plastic particles <5mm contaminating water, soil, and organisms globally

concepts.label: Nanoplastic
concepts.definition: Plastic particles <1μm capable of crossing cellular membranes

concepts.label: Great Pacific Garbage Patch
concepts.definition: Massive accumulation of marine debris in North Pacific, ~1.6 million km²

concepts.label: Marine Debris
concepts.definition: Human-created waste in oceans including plastics, fishing gear, and packaging

concepts.label: Ghost Net
concepts.definition: Abandoned fishing net that continues to entangle and kill marine life

concepts.label: Thermal Pollution
concepts.definition: Degradation of water quality by changing its ambient temperature

concepts.label: Noise Pollution
concepts.definition: Harmful environmental sound from traffic, industry, or sonar affecting wildlife

concepts.label: Light Pollution
concepts.definition: Excessive artificial light disrupting ecosystems and astronomical observation

concepts.label: Soil Pollution
concepts.definition: Contamination of soil with chemicals, heavy metals, or waste products

concepts.label: Pesticide Pollution
concepts.definition: Environmental contamination from agricultural chemicals killing non-target organisms

concepts.label: Herbicide Pollution
concepts.definition: Contamination from chemical weed killers affecting soil and water quality

concepts.label: Fertilizer Runoff
concepts.definition: Agricultural nutrient pollution entering waterways causing eutrophication

concepts.label: Pharmaceutical Pollution
concepts.definition: Drug residues in waterways disrupting aquatic organisms and ecosystems

concepts.label: PFAS Contamination
concepts.definition: Persistent 'forever chemicals' contaminating water and accumulating in organisms

concepts.label: Radioactive Pollution
concepts.definition: Contamination from nuclear waste, accidents, or weapons testing

concepts.label: E-Waste
concepts.definition: Discarded electronic devices releasing toxic metals and chemicals

concepts.label: Landfill
concepts.definition: Waste disposal site producing methane and leachate pollution

concepts.label: Incineration
concepts.definition: Burning waste releasing dioxins, furans, and particulates

concepts.label: Bioaccumulation
concepts.definition: Gradual accumulation of toxins in an organism over its lifetime

concepts.label: Biomagnification
concepts.definition: Increasing concentration of toxins at higher trophic levels in food chains

concepts.label: DDT
concepts.definition: Persistent organochlorine pesticide banned in many countries, bioaccumulates

concepts.label: Chlorofluorocarbons
concepts.definition: Synthetic chemicals (CFCs) that deplete stratospheric ozone, phased out by Montreal Protocol

concepts.label: Endocrine Disruptor
concepts.definition: Chemical that interferes with hormonal systems in wildlife and humans

concepts.label: El Niño
concepts.definition: Warming of eastern equatorial Pacific causing global weather disruptions every 2-7 years

concepts.label: La Niña
concepts.definition: Cooling of eastern equatorial Pacific causing opposite effects to El Niño

concepts.label: ENSO
concepts.definition: El Niño-Southern Oscillation: coupled ocean-atmosphere climate pattern in Pacific

concepts.label: Monsoon
concepts.definition: Seasonal wind reversal bringing heavy rainfall, especially in South and Southeast Asia

concepts.label: Indian Monsoon
concepts.definition: Major seasonal rain system bringing 70-90% of India's annual rainfall

concepts.label: East Asian Monsoon
concepts.definition: Seasonal monsoon affecting China, Korea, and Japan with summer rains

concepts.label: West African Monsoon
concepts.definition: Seasonal rain system critical for Sahel agriculture

concepts.label: North Atlantic Oscillation
concepts.definition: Climate pattern of atmospheric pressure difference between Iceland and Azores

concepts.label: Arctic Oscillation
concepts.definition: Climate pattern of atmospheric pressure in the Arctic affecting mid-latitude weather

concepts.label: Pacific Decadal Oscillation
concepts.definition: Long-term (20-30 year) pattern of Pacific climate variability

concepts.label: Atlantic Multidecadal Oscillation
concepts.definition: Cycle of North Atlantic sea surface temperatures over 60-80 years

concepts.label: Indian Ocean Dipole
concepts.definition: Temperature oscillation between western and eastern Indian Ocean affecting rainfall

concepts.label: Madden-Julian Oscillation
concepts.definition: Eastward-moving tropical weather disturbance cycling every 30-60 days

concepts.label: Southern Annular Mode
concepts.definition: North-south movement of westerly wind belt around Antarctica

concepts.label: Polar Vortex
concepts.definition: Large area of low pressure and cold air surrounding Earth's poles

concepts.label: Jet Stream
concepts.definition: Fast-flowing atmospheric air current influencing weather patterns

concepts.label: Thermohaline Circulation
concepts.definition: Global ocean conveyor belt driven by temperature and salinity differences

concepts.label: AMOC
concepts.definition: Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation transporting heat northward, weakening

concepts.label: Gulf Stream
concepts.definition: Warm Atlantic ocean current moderating European climate

concepts.label: Upwelling
concepts.definition: Rising of cold, nutrient-rich deep water to surface, supports fisheries

concepts.label: Albedo Effect
concepts.definition: Reflection of solar radiation; ice and snow have high albedo, dark surfaces low

concepts.label: Ice-Albedo Feedback
concepts.definition: Positive feedback: melting ice reduces reflectivity, increasing warming

concepts.label: Permafrost Thaw
concepts.definition: Melting of permanently frozen ground releasing stored methane and CO₂

concepts.label: Methane Clathrate
concepts.definition: Frozen methane trapped in ice crystals on ocean floor and in permafrost

concepts.label: Carbon Cycle
concepts.definition: Biogeochemical cycle of carbon exchange between atmosphere, oceans, land, and organisms

concepts.label: Nitrogen Cycle
concepts.definition: Biogeochemical cycle of nitrogen through atmosphere, soil, water, and organisms

concepts.label: Water Cycle
concepts.definition: Continuous movement of water through evaporation, precipitation, and runoff

concepts.label: Phosphorus Cycle
concepts.definition: Biogeochemical cycle of phosphorus through rocks, soil, water, and organisms

concepts.label: Carbon Sink
concepts.definition: Natural or artificial reservoir absorbing more carbon than it releases

concepts.label: Carbon Source
concepts.definition: Process or reservoir releasing more carbon than it absorbs

concepts.label: Tipping Point
concepts.definition: Critical threshold beyond which a system reorganizes irreversibly

concepts.label: Climate Feedback
concepts.definition: Process that amplifies or dampens climate change effects

concepts.label: Urban Heat Island
concepts.definition: Urban areas significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas

concepts.label: Heat Wave
concepts.definition: Prolonged period of excessively hot weather, increasing in frequency

concepts.label: Drought
concepts.definition: Extended period of below-normal precipitation causing water shortage

concepts.label: Megadrought
concepts.definition: Prolonged drought lasting decades, exacerbated by climate change

concepts.label: Wildfire
concepts.definition: Uncontrolled fire in natural areas, intensifying with climate change

concepts.label: Crown Fire
concepts.definition: Forest fire that spreads through treetop canopy, extremely destructive

concepts.label: Firestorm
concepts.definition: Intense fire generating its own wind system, extremely dangerous

concepts.label: Prescribed Burn
concepts.definition: Controlled fire used for ecosystem management and fuel reduction

concepts.label: Flood
concepts.definition: Overflow of water submerging normally dry land

concepts.label: Flash Flood
concepts.definition: Sudden local flood from intense rainfall, extremely dangerous

concepts.label: Coastal Flooding
concepts.definition: Inundation of coastal land by storm surges, tides, or sea level rise

concepts.label: River Flood
concepts.definition: Overflow of river banks from prolonged rainfall or snowmelt

concepts.label: Glacial Lake Outburst Flood
concepts.definition: Sudden release of water from a glacier-dammed lake (GLOF)

concepts.label: Hurricane
concepts.definition: Tropical cyclone with sustained winds >119 km/h in Atlantic/East Pacific

concepts.label: Typhoon
concepts.definition: Tropical cyclone with sustained winds >119 km/h in Northwest Pacific

concepts.label: Cyclone
concepts.definition: Tropical cyclone in the Indian Ocean and South Pacific

concepts.label: Tropical Storm
concepts.definition: Organized tropical system with winds 63-118 km/h

concepts.label: Storm Surge
concepts.definition: Abnormal rise of water generated by a storm above predicted tide

concepts.label: Tornado
concepts.definition: Violent rotating column of air in contact with ground, wind speeds up to 480 km/h

concepts.label: Waterspout
concepts.definition: Tornado occurring over water, weaker than land tornadoes

concepts.label: Derecho
concepts.definition: Widespread, long-lived straight-line windstorm associated with band of thunderstorms

concepts.label: Blizzard
concepts.definition: Severe snowstorm with strong winds >56 km/h and low visibility

concepts.label: Ice Storm
concepts.definition: Storm with freezing rain coating surfaces in ice, damaging infrastructure

concepts.label: Hailstorm
concepts.definition: Storm producing ice pellets that can damage crops and property

concepts.label: Thunderstorm
concepts.definition: Storm with lightning, thunder, and heavy precipitation

concepts.label: Supercell
concepts.definition: Severe thunderstorm with deep, rotating updraft capable of producing tornadoes

concepts.label: Dust Storm
concepts.definition: Strong wind carrying large amounts of dust reducing visibility near zero

concepts.label: Sandstorm
concepts.definition: Wind transporting sand particles, common in desert regions

concepts.label: Haboob
concepts.definition: Intense dust storm caused by strong convective downdraft

concepts.label: Earthquake
concepts.definition: Sudden release of energy in Earth's crust causing seismic waves

concepts.label: Tsunami
concepts.definition: Series of ocean waves caused by submarine earthquakes, landslides, or volcanic eruptions

concepts.label: Volcanic Eruption
concepts.definition: Expulsion of magma, gases, and ash from a volcano

concepts.label: Pyroclastic Flow
concepts.definition: Fast-moving current of hot gas and volcanic matter, extremely lethal

concepts.label: Lahar
concepts.definition: Destructive mudflow of volcanic debris and water on volcanic slopes

concepts.label: Volcanic Winter
concepts.definition: Global cooling from volcanic ash and sulfur aerosols blocking sunlight

concepts.label: Landslide
concepts.definition: Downslope movement of rock, earth, or debris under gravity

concepts.label: Mudslide
concepts.definition: Fast-moving flow of mud and debris, often triggered by heavy rain

concepts.label: Rockfall
concepts.definition: Detachment and rapid descent of rock fragments from steep slopes

concepts.label: Avalanche
concepts.definition: Rapid flow of snow and ice down a mountain slope

concepts.label: Sinkhole
concepts.definition: Ground collapse from dissolution of underlying soluble rock

concepts.label: Subsidence
concepts.definition: Gradual sinking of ground surface from groundwater extraction or mining

concepts.label: Liquefaction
concepts.definition: Soil losing strength during earthquakes, behaving as liquid

concepts.label: Seiche
concepts.definition: Standing wave oscillation in an enclosed water body from seismic or wind forces

concepts.label: Limnic Eruption
concepts.definition: Sudden release of dissolved CO₂ from deep lake water, suffocating nearby life

concepts.label: Geomagnetic Storm
concepts.definition: Disturbance of Earth's magnetosphere from solar wind affecting electronics

concepts.label: Solar Flare
concepts.definition: Sudden flash of brightness on Sun emitting electromagnetic radiation

concepts.label: Asteroid Impact
concepts.definition: Collision of celestial body with Earth causing devastation, mass extinction risk

concepts.label: Supervolcano
